subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the whimsical and mysterious nature of the 'Funny Man' (Mr. Nobody)
Identify the cause-and-effect of common household mischief
Learn the structure of a humorous poem and its rhyming pattern
Recognize the use of personification in portraying an invisible character
Develop recitation skills with proper rhythm and expression
Practice using common household vocabulary and action verbs

lesson_plan.title: Who is Mr. Nobody?
topics_covered: Introduction to the poem and its title
teaching_strategy: Class discussion: 'Who gets blamed when things go missing at home?'
Introduce the character of Mr. Nobody as a quiet, invisible man
Predictive brainstorming: What kind of funny things does he do?
student_activities: Share funny incidents of broken things or misplaced items at home
Draw what they think 'Mr. Nobody' might look like if he were visible

lesson_plan.title: Poem Recitation and Flow
topics_covered: Reading the poem with the correct rhythm
Understanding the literal meaning of each stanza
teaching_strategy: Model reading by the teacher to show the 'nursery rhyme' beat
Choral reading (boys read one stanza, girls read the next)
Explaining the simple narrative of the poem
student_activities: Read the poem aloud in pairs
Highlight all the action verbs (cracked, broke, mislaid)

lesson_plan.title: The List of Mischief
topics_covered: Linking 'Mr. Nobody' to broken plates and scattered pins
Understanding that everyone 'blames' this funny man
teaching_strategy: Teacher-led discussion on the 'cracked plates' and 'torn books'
Use real objects (a plate, a book, a pin) to illustrate the poem
Explain the phrase 'He does the mischief that is done'
student_activities: List all the 'funny' crimes committed by Mr. Nobody in the poem
Match the object to the damage (e.g., Plate -> Cracked)

lesson_plan.title: Rhyme and Rhythm
teaching_strategy: Highlighting rhyming words on the board using different colors
Clapping along to the rhythm of the stanzas
Introduction to the 'AABB' or 'ABCB' rhyme scheme
student_activities: Find the rhyming partner for words hidden in the classroom

lesson_plan.title: The Moral: Taking Responsibility
topics_covered: The humor behind blaming someone else
The difference between 'Mr. Nobody' and ourselves
teaching_strategy: Discussion: 'Is there really a Mr. Nobody?'
Exploring why the poet calls him 'funny' and 'quiet'
Relating the poem to personal accountability
student_activities: Write a 3-line 'confession' for a time they accidentally broke something
Share why it's funny that we all agree 'Mr. Nobody' did it

lesson_plan.title: Fun and Revision
teaching_strategy: Vocabulary Bingo using words from the poem
Final quiz on the 'crimes' of Mr. Nobody
student_activities: Perform the poem with funny gestures for each 'mischief'

teaching_aids: Props: A cracked plate (safe), a torn paper, a pin, a boot
teacher_tip: Keep the energy light and humorous! When students list mischief, ask them with a wink, 'Was it Mr. Nobody, or was it you?' This helps them connect with the irony and wit of the poem.
